rulings.Case Title: FARHAD ZAGHI VS. CECELIA KENNEDY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant And Cross-Complainant Cecelia Kennedys Notice Of Motion, And Motion, For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 27, 2018 line 1. DEFENDANT CECELIA KENNEDY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY Adjudication GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. (a) Motion is DENIED as to all issues seeking determination of breach. Paramount Petroleum Corp v. Sup. Ct. (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 226. Moving party's reliance on People ex. rel. Feuer. Sup. Ct. (2015) 234 Cal.App.4th 1360 is misplaced. Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that any causes of action asserted against the defendants (and on which adjudication of breach of duty is sought) do not contain "damages" as an element of the cause of action. Additionally, Plaintiff admits that "damages" are not a mere remedy; they are an element of a cause of action. E.g. Memorandum of Points and Authorities 7:1. (b) Motion for Summary Adjudication of issue # 13 (re: duty not to proceed with a claim against the victim of identity theft after receiving facts regarding identity theft) is DENIED. Objections to Moving Party's Ex. I are sustained. Moving party failed to shift its burden and show that Zaghi received facts regarding identity theft. At most, admissible evidence shows that Zaghi received notice that Kennedy did not receive the full amount of the loan; and there might have been fraud, not necessarily identity theft. (c) Motion for Summary Adjudications of issues as to the existence of duty as to Stewart Title is GRANTED as follows: Issues 1 and 2: are not opposed. Issue 3: Stewart Title had a duty of neutrality in conducting the escrow." Issue 4: Stewart Title has a duty not to dispose of escrow property except in compliance with Escrow Instructions to the extent instruction re: Disposition of the property were contained in the Escrow Instructions. Issue 9: Stewart Title had a fiduciary duty to strictly comply with escrow instructions. Issue 10: Stewart Title had a duty 'to communicate to his principal knowledge acquired in the course of his agency with respect to material facts which might affect the principal's decision as to a pending transaction, particularly where ... he knows that the principal is looking to him for protection as to those very facts of which he has knowledge.' The Court notes that Moving Party seeks adjudication of existence of duty by using broad clearly established black-letter law statements without applying any specific facts, e.g. the Court is not asked to adjudicate whether Stewart Title acquired knowledge of material facts, whether those material facts might have affected moving party's decision, etc. Therefore, Motion is GRANTED based only on the issues (1,2,3,4, 9, and 10) as framed by the notice and by the sole undisputed material fact, i.e. no determination is made as to the specific narrow issues in this case (whatever they may be). = (501/CFH)

Moving party fails to sustain his burden under CCP 473(b). Moving party and its counsel are ordered to comply with SFLR 2.7B in future. = (501/CFH)
